title: Social Insurance Number in MSCA - Canada.ca
content:
## Social Insurance Number in MSCA
You can view and print your Social Insurance Number (SIN) in My Service Canada Account (MSCA). Once registered or signed in to MSCA, you will be able to view your:
- names as they appear on the SIN record
- SIN expiry date (if applicable)
SIN applications are submitted using a separate method. To apply for a SIN and for more information, visit Social Insurance Number .

## How to view your SIN in MSCA
To view your SIN in MSCA, select the “Register or Sign in to MSCA” button.
Once signed in or registered, go to the Social Insurance Number section on your MSCA dashboard.
To register, you must be at least 12 years old and only access MSCA as yourself. No one is authorized to use or create an account for you.
Register or Sign in to MSCA

### If you don’t have an account
Before registering, identify which situation applies to you and which documents you need.
To register for MSCA, you will need to:
- verify your identity with one of the following: the Interac ® verification service, or your personal access code (PAC)
- the Interac ® verification service, or
- your personal access code (PAC)
To register for MSCA, you will need to:
- provide one of the following: Birth Registration Number found on your birth certificate, or Unique Client Identifier (UCI), also known as client identification number (client ID) issued by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada
- Birth Registration Number found on your birth certificate, or
- Unique Client Identifier (UCI), also known as client identification number (client ID) issued by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada
- verify your identity with the Interac ® verification service
Your SIN will be available in MSCA once your online SIN application has been processed (normally within 10 business days of your online application). Before registering for MSCA, make sure you have received an email confirming your application has been processed.
To register for MSCA, you will need one of the following:
- Birth Registration Number found on your Canadian birth certificate, or
- Unique Client Identifier (UCI), also known as client identification (client ID) number issued by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada
You will then need to verify your identity. You can use one of the following:
- your SIN Application Number (provided in the SIN application confirmation email), or
- the Interac ® verification service

#### What is a SIN Application Number is
- It is a 12-character number that you receive after submitting your online SIN application
- You must be at least 12 years old and have applied for a SIN online yourself to receive a SIN Application Number
- You can use it to verify your identity when registering for MSCA
- It is only valid for MSCA registration once your online SIN application has been processed. You will receive a confirmation email informing you about the status of your application
- It will expire 3 days after your SIN application has been processed

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
